SESSION.NAME: defaultSession
SESSION.DESCRIPTION: This diagnostic session enables the default diagnostic session in the server(s) and does not support any diagnostic application timeout handling provisions (e.g., TesterPresent service is not necessary to keep the session active). A server shall always start the default diagnostic session when powered up. If no other diagnostic session is started, then the default diagnostic session shall be running as long as the server is powered.

SESSION.NAME: programmingSession
SESSION.DESCRIPTION: This diagnosticSession enables all diagnostic services required to support the memory programming of a server. Regardless of whether the programmingSession runs in boot software or not, the ECU shall automatically suspend logging of DTCs and suspend transmission of non-diagnostic frames when entering the programmingSession.

SESSION.NAME: extendedDiagnosticSession
SESSION.DESCRIPTION: This diagnosticSession is used to enable timed diagnostic service functionality (e.g., I/O control). The diagnostic functionality in this session shall also include all supported diagnostic functionality supported in the defaultSession.

DATA_FORMAT_IDENTIFIER.NAME: No Compression Method and No Encryption Method

Circuit Short To Battery or Open

DTC_SNAPSHOT_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: The first time since DTC information was cleared or the DTC was aged that DTC status bit 3 (confirmedDTC) is set to 1.

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: Operation cycle counter #1
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: Number of operation cycles since the DTC fault detection counter last reached its maximum value of +127 (since DTC information was last cleared). All operation cycles, including those during which the test was not completed shall be included.

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: Operation cycle counter #2
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: Number of operation cycles since the DTC fault detection counter last reached its maximum value of +127 (since DTC information was last cleared). The operation cycles during which the test was not completed shall be excluded

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: Operation cycle counter #3
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: Number of operation cycles since the DTC fault detection counter first reached its maximum value of +127 (since DTC information was last cleared). All operation cycles, including those in which the test has not been completed shall be included.

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: Operation cycle counter #4
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: Number of operation cycles during which the DTC fault detection counter reached its maximum value of +127 (since DTC information was last cleared).

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: DTC Fault Detection Counter
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: The purpose of this counter is to provide a mechanism for filtering the results of a low-level fault detection process so that test results (pass and fail) can be qualified before setting any DTC status bits.

DTC_OPERATION_CYCLES_SUPPORTED.DTC_OPERATION_CYCLE: Monitoring begins with BECM is powered on by EV_WAKEUP signal either from HPCM (Pin LV1_4), or CAN message from BCCM (battery charger), or self-sustained
Monitoring begins with BECM is powered on by EV_WAKEUP signal either from HPCM (Pin LV1_4), or CAN message from BCCM (battery charger), or self-sustained.
Monitoring begins with BECM is powered on by EV_WAKEUP signal or self-sustained.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: System Voltage High

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: At power up, before BECM is commanding any contactors being closed
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose main positive contactor weld fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: At power up, before BECM is commanding any contactors being closed b) Malfunction Condition: BECM HWIO feedback indicates main positive contactor weld c) Confirmation Time: Event based Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set P0563-00 System Voltage High b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Transmit BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = 0x5: Fault if conditions for req[PEV BECM ACCHRG FUNC 05] or req[PEV BECM DCCHRG FUNC 05] is satisfied d) Inhibit start for the remaining of the power cycle e) Request the MIL to be illuminated. This is a one trip MIL. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Failure results in vehicle not start
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Warning Light.

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Reduce both long term and short term charging/discharging power limits to 5kW.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Internal Control Module EEPROM Error

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is reading or writing the EEPROM
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnostic information read/write EEPROM malfunction per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is reading or writing the EEPROM b) Malfunction Condition: BECM HWIO feedback indicates EEPROM malfunction detected. Can be read/write error, checksum error, etc., that prevents BECM read/write pending code information and read battery calibration values c) Confirmation Time: Event based Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set P062F-00 Internal Control Module EEPROM Error b) Request service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated. This is a one-trip MIL. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Failure will result in pending code cannot be written into module memory. Will disable the two-trip MIL setting. b) Failure will cause BECM to use default calibration values, reduce all-electric range
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Unauthorized Software/Calibration Detected

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: a) Entry Condition: BECM is powered on
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall set a permanent Unauthorized Software/Calibration Detected in M-code Application Software per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational and SW is M-code b) Malfunction Condition: N/A c) Confirmation Time: Event based Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) DTC P064F-00: Unauthorized Software/Calibration Detected. b) Request Warning light to be illuminated The BECM shall set a permanent Unauthorized Software/Calibration Detected in In Plant Flash (IPF) Software per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational and SW is IPF b) Malfunction Condition: N/A c) Confirmation Time: Event based Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) DTC P064F-00: Unauthorized Software/Calibration Detected b) Request Warning light to be illuminated Permanent DTC means that the DTC will be set by default. The DTC will be re-set immediately after clear DTC command has been processed.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: N/A

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Motor Electronics Coolant Pump "A" Control Circuit Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: CONT_PWR is high
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose MECP short to ground or open circuit fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: CONT_PWR is high b) Malfunction Condition: the feedback signal BECM receives from MECP HWIO indicates wire fault open circuit or short to ground c) Confirmation Time: 30 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0A06-00 Motor Electronics Coolant Pump Control Circuit Low b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. In addition, BECM shall command pump speed to 100% during ODST, and reduce the fault confirmation time to 15 seconds, calibratable. Within the power cycle, if the HWIO indicates the fault is no longer present, BECM shall take 30 seconds maturity time to recover from the fault, stop requesting the service light, and set HtrnClntPump_D_Stat to an appropriate non-fault state. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Component is used as part of the thermal management system. b) BECM cannot distinguish the difference between open circuit and short to ground faults
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Motor Electronics Coolant Pump "A" Control Circuit High

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: CONT_PWR is high
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose MECP short to LV battery fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: CONT_PWR is high b) Malfunction Condition: the feedback signal BECM receives from MECP HWIO indicates wire fault short to LV battery c) Confirmation Time: 30 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0A07-00 Motor Electronics Coolant Pump Control Circuit High b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. In addition, BECM shall command pump speed to 100% during ODST, and reduce the fault confirmation time to 15 seconds, calibratable. Within the power cycle, if the HWIO indicates the fault is no longer present, BECM shall take 30 seconds maturity time to recover from the fault, stop requesting the service light, and set HtrnClntPump_D_Stat to an appropriate non-fault state. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Component is used as part of the thermal management system.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: High Voltage System Interlock Circuit "A"

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose SBAR_IN short to low voltage battery circuit faulted per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction Condition: SBAR_IN pin short to low voltage battery fault is detected c) Confirmation Time: 50 milliseconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0A0A-00 High Voltage System Interlock Circuit "A" b) Request warning light to be illuminated c) Set inhibit start for the remaining of the power cycle d) Transmit BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = 0x5: Fault if conditions for req[PEV BECM ACCHRG FUNC 05] or req[PEV BECM DCCHRG FUNC 05] is satisfied e) Reduce both long term and short term charging/discharging power limits to 5kW (FHEV/PHEV) or 5 kW (BEV) BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle, if the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles This fault does not result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Safety only component
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Power Limiting. Inibit Start. No Charging.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Warning Light.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: High Voltage System Interlock Circuit "A" Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose battery pack interlock circuit open or short to ground per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction Condition: The HWIO feedback indicates interlock circuit is open; or HWIO returns SBAR_IN short to ground fault c) Confirmation Time: 50 milliseconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0A0C-00 High Voltage System Interlock Circuit "A" Low b) Request warning light to be illuminated c) Set inhibit start for the remaining of the power cycle d) Transmit BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = 0x5: Fault if conditions for req[PEV BECM ACCHRG FUNC 05] or req[BEV BECM DCCHRG FUNC 05] is satisfied e) Reduce both long term and short term charging/discharging power limits to 5kW (FHEV/PHEV) or 5kW (BEV) BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle, if the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles; Or BECM shall clear the fault when the state transition of Ignition_Status = 0x1: Off followed by Ignition_Status = 0x4: Run or Ignition_Status = 0x8: Start has occurred. This fault does not result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Safety only component src[eTracker 15049305]
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Power Limiting. Inibit Start. No Charging.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Warning Light.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack State of Charge Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational, battery pack voltage or SOC is valid
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose battery pack over-discharge per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational, battery pack voltage or SOC is valid b) Malfunction Condition: The instantaneous SOC of the battery pack is lower than a calibratable threshold (different thresholds for contactors open and closed scenarios); or the battery pack voltage is lower than a calibratable threshold (different thresholds for contactors open and closed scenarios). c) Confirmation Time: 85 seconds for the contactor open scenario, 9 seconds for contactor closed scenario, both calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0A7D-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Pack State of Charge Low b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Perform normal BPO d) Disable battery cell balancing e) Request the MIL to be illuminated. This is one trip MIL. BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Failure will results in battery power off, impacts electric motor propulsion.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Battery Power Off/Shut-down. No Charging. No Cell Balancing.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Warning Light.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack "A" Over Temperature

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose battery temperature high per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction Condition: Battery temperature is higher than 65 degree C, calibratable c) Confirmation Time: 1 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0A7E-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Over Temperature b) Perform normal BPO c) Request the warning light to be illuminated BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault does not result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Diagnostic monitors exist for corresponding sensor components. b) Failure can be caused by external environment.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Warning Light.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack "A" Deterioration

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose battery end-of-life per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction Condition: battery end of life detection counter is greater than or equal to a calibratable threshold c) Confirmation Time: Event based Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0A7F-00 Battery Pack "A" Deterioration b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall latch this fault until a new BECM software re-flash. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Indicates the hybrid battery state of health.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational, and TSL measurement is valid
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose TSL sensor rationality fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational, and TSL measurement is valid b) Malfunction Condition: the difference between the measurement reading of one temperature sensor and the average reading of the remaining temperature sensors is beyond a calibratable range. c) Confirmation Time: 5 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set appropriate DTC BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault does not result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) No action taken to this fault. b) All Diagnostic Monitors complete normally. c) Charging performance not affected. d) Electric motor propulsion not affected. e) Regen Braking not affected. f) Emissions not affected

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose TSL sensor circuit faulted low per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction Condition: the reported temperature sensor reading is lower than a calibratable threshold c) Confirmation Time: 5 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set appropriate DTC BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit High

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose TSL sensor circuit faulted high or open circuit per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction Condition: the reported temperature sensor reading is greater than a calibratable threshold c) Confirmation Time: 5 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set appropriate DTC BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault does not result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) No action taken to this fault. b) All Diagnostic Monitors complete normally. c) Charging performance not affected. d) Electric motor propulsion not affected. e) Regen Braking not affected. f) Emissions not affected

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Positive Contactor "A" Circuit Stuck Closed

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding main positive contactor to be opened, BECM evaluated battery pack voltage is valid, and V_CONT_POS is not faulted, and the conditions listed in req[XEV BECM CONTACT FMEM 09] is not satisfied, battery pack voltage is greater than a calibratable value
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose main positive contactor stuck closed per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding main positive contactor to be opened, BECM evaluated battery pack voltage is valid, and V_CONT_POS is not faulted, and the conditions listed in req[XEV BECM CONTACT FMEM 09] is not satisfied, battery pack voltage is greater than a calibratable value b) Malfunction Condition: the difference between BECM evaluated battery pack voltage and V_CONT_POS voltage is within a calibratable threshold c) Confirmation Time: 60 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0AA1-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Positive Contactor Circuit Stuck Closed b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Transmit BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = 0x5: Fault if conditions for req[PEV BECM ACCHRG FUNC 05] or req[PEV BECM DCCHRG FUNC 05] is satisfied d) Inhibit start for the remaining of the power cycle e) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Main positive contactor malfunction affects electric propulsion The BECM shall diagnose main positive contactor weld fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: At power up, before BECM is commanding any contactors being closed b) Malfunction Condition: BECM HWIO feedback indicates main positive contactor weld c) Confirmation Time: Event based Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set P0AA1-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Positive Contactor Circuit Stuck Closed b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Transmit BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = 0x5: Fault if conditions for req[PEV BECM ACCHRG FUNC 05] or req[PEV BECM DCCHRG FUNC 05] is satisfied d) Inhibit start for the remaining of the power cycle e) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Failure results in vehicle not start
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Warning Light.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Positive Contactor "A" Circuit Stuck Open

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding main positive contactor to be closed, BECM evaluated battery pack voltage is valid, V_CONT_POS or HtrnAin_UHi_Actl is not faulted, main positive contactor coil current is higher than a calibratable value
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose main positive contactor stuck open per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding main positive contactor to be closed, BECM evaluated battery pack voltage is valid, V_CONT_POS or HtrnAin_UHi_Actl is not faulted, main positive contactor coil current is higher than a calibratable value b) Malfunction Condition: the difference between BECM evaluated battery pack voltage and V_CONT_POS voltage (or HtrnAin_UHi_Actl) is beyond a calibratable threshold, or battery pack voltage is lower than a calibratable value, i.e., BECM evaluated positive contactor status is open c) Confirmation Time: 2.4 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0AA2-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Positive Contactor Circuit Stuck Open b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Perform normal BPO d) Transmit BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = 0x5: Fault if conditions for req[PEV BECM ACCHRG FUNC 05] or req[PEV BECM DCCHRG FUNC 05] is satisfied e) Request the MIL to be illuminated. This is one trip MIL. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle, if the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles; Or BECM shall clear the fault if a quick key cycle has occurred. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Main positive contactor malfunction affects electric propulsion.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Warning Light.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Negative Contactor "A" Circuit Stuck Closed

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding main negative contactor to be open, and the conditions listed in req[XEV BECM CONTACT FMEM 09] is not satisfied, and V_CONT_NEG measurement is valid
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose main negative contactor stuck closed per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding main negative contactor to be open, and the conditions listed in req[XEV BECM CONTACT FMEM 09] is not satisfied, and V_CONT_NEG measurement is valid b) Malfunction Condition: BECM HWIO feedback reports main negative contactor is closed c) Confirmation Time: 60 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0AA4-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Negative Contactor Circuit Stuck Closed b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall clear the fault if the malfunction condition is no longer present. BECM shall carry over the fault between power cycles if the malfunction condition still presents at BECM power off. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Main negative contactor malfunction affects electric propulsion
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Negative Contactor "A" Circuit Stuck Open

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding main negative contactor to be closed, battery pack voltage is greater than 75V, calibratable, and MAIN_NEG_CONT HWIO reports coil current is higher than a calibratable threshold
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose main negative contactor stuck open per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding main negative contactor to be closed, battery pack voltage is greater than 75V, calibratable, and MAIN_NEG_CONT HWIO reports coil current is higher than a calibratable threshold b) Malfunction Condition: BECM HWIO feedback reports main negative contactor is open, and HtrnAin_UHi_Actl is outside 10% of battery pack voltage, i.e., BECM evaluated negative contactor status is open. c) Confirmation Time: 2.4 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0AA5-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Negative Contactor Circuit Stuck Open b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Perform normal BPO d) Request the MIL to be illuminated. This is one trip MIL. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle, if the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles; Or BECM shall clear the fault if a quick key cycle has occurred. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Main negative contactor malfunction affects electric propulsion.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Warning Light.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack "A" Voltage System Isolation Fault

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational, leakage detection circuit is not faulted, and DC charge contactors are being commanded open
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose leakage fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational, leakage detection circuit is not faulted, and DC charge contactors are being commanded open b) Malfunction Condition: the leakage resistance measurement indicates mild or severe leakage fault exists c) Confirmation Time: three out of five detects, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set P0AA6-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage System Isolation Fault b) Request service light to be illuminated c) Set no-restart fault for severe leakage. d) Transmit BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = 0x5: Fault if conditions for req[PEV BECM ACCHRG FUNC 05] or req[BEV BECM DCCHRG FUNC 05] is satisfied, for severe leakage fault e) Disables battery cell balancing for severe leakage. BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. Instead of three out of five failded detections, BECM shall reduce the confirmation strategy to return DTC with only one failed detection. For mild leakage fault, The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. For severe leakage fault, The BECM shall latch the fault until a technician clear this DTC. This fault does not result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Safety only component
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: No Restart. No Charging. No Cell Balancing.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack "A" Voltage Isolation Sensor Circuit

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: At BECM power up
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose leakage detection circuit fail per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: At BECM power up b) Malfunction Condition: Leakage detection circuit check does not pass for 2 consecutive times c) Confirmation Time: Event based Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set P0AA7-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage Isolation Sensor Circuit b) Request service light to be illuminated c) Transmit BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = 0x5: Fault if conditions for req[PEV BECM ACCHRG FUNC 05] or req[BEV BECM DCCHRG FUNC 05] is satisfied BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault does not result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Safety only component
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack "A" Voltage Sense "A" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: Cell voltage and pack voltage measurements are available, HWIO does not report any unmonitored cell voltages, and V_TOP circuit fault is not present
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose battery pack voltage sensing circuit rationality fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: Cell voltage and pack voltage measurements are available, HWIO does not report any unmonitored cell voltages, and V_TOP circuit fault is not present b) Malfunction Condition: the difference between difference between the summation of cell voltages and V_TOP measurement is greater than 50V, calibratable c) Confirmation Time: 5 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0ABB-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Voltage Sense "A" Circuit Range/Performance b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Sensor failure results in losing the capability to monitor battery state of health
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose battery pack voltage sensing circuit fault low per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction Condition: the measured battery pack voltage by BECM is out of range low c) Confirmation Time: 1 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0ABC-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Voltage Sense "A" Circuit Low b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. This fault does not result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) No action taken to this fault. b) All Diagnostic Monitors complete normally. c) Charging performance not affected. d) Electric motor propulsion not affected. e) Regen Braking not affected. f) Emissions not affected

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Positive Contactor Control Circuit/Open

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose MAIN_POS_CONT circuit faulted per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction Condition: MAIN_POS_CONT HWIO feedback reports contactor low side driver circuit fault has been detected c) Confirmation Time: 1 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0AD9-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Positive Contactor Control Circuit/Open GM b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle, if the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Main positive contactor malfunction affects electric propulsion.

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose MAIN_POS_CONT coil overcurrent per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding main positive contactor to be closed b) Malfunction Condition: MAIN_POS_CONT HWIO reports coil overcurrent state c) Confirmation Time: 20 milliseconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0ADC-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Positive Contactor Control Circuit High b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Perform normal BPO d) Request the MIL to be illuminated. This is one trip MIL. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Negative Contactor "A" Control Circuit High

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding main negative contactor to be closed
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose MAIN_NEG_CONT short circuit per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding main negative contactor to be closed b) Malfunction Condition: MAIN_NEG_CONT HWIO reports coil overcurrent state c) Confirmation Time: 20 milliseconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0AE0-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Negative Contactor Control Circuit High b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Perform normal BPO d) Inhibit start for the remaining of the power cycle e) Request the MIL to be illuminated. This is one trip MIL. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Main negative contactor malfunction affects electric propulsion.

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding precharge contactor to be closed
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose PRECHARGE_CONT coil overcurrent fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding precharge contactor to be closed b) Malfunction Condition: PRECHARGE_CONT HWIO reports coil overcurrent state c) Confirmation Time: 20 milliseconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0AE7-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Precharge Contactor Control Circuit "A" High b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Terminate current contactor close sequence d) Inhibit start for the remaining of the power cycle e) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Precharge malfunction results in main contactor cannot be closed.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Temperature Sensor "D" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational, and TSL measurement is valid
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose TSL sensor rationality fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational, and TSL measurement is valid b) Malfunction Condition: the difference between the measurement reading of one temperature sensor and the average reading of the remaining temperature sensors is beyond a calibratable range. c) Confirmation Time: 5 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set appropriate DTC BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault does not result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) No action taken to this fault. b) All Diagnostic Monitors complete normally. c) Charging performance not affected. d) Electric motor propulsion not affected. e) Regen Braking not affected. f) Emissions not affected

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery System Voltage High

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational, and battery pack voltage is valid
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose battery pack overcharge per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational, and battery pack voltage is valid b) Malfunction Condition: the battery pack voltage is greater than a calibratable threshold c) Confirmation Time: 10 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0AFB-00 Hybrid/EV Battery System Voltage High b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Perform normal BPO d) Transmit BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = 0x5: Fault if conditions for req[PEV BECM ACCHRG FUNC 05] or req[BEV BECM DCCHRG FUNC 05] is satisfied e) Request the MIL to be illuminated. This is one trip MIL. BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Failure will results in battery power off, impacts electric motor propulsion. This requirement is a special case for req[XEV BECM DIAG FUNC 115] and req[XEV BECM DIAG FUNC 117]. If BECM detects ChrgrInPwMde_D_Actl = AcBasic || AcDigital || DcCharging, BECM shall use bppc_cell_fail_noload_tmr and bppc_pck_sev_od_v_time_noload, respectively, for instantaneous SOC based cell/pack over discharge protection when contactors are closed. If BECM detects ChrgrInPwMde_D_Actl = AcBasic || AcDigital || DcCharging, and the 10-second average battery current is less than -1A, calibratable, i.e, current is flowing into the battery, BECM shall turn off the instantaneous SOC based cell/pack over discharge protection when contactors are closed. In any other cases, including high range current sensor failure (req[xEV BECM DIAG FUNC 24], req[xEV BECM DIAG FUNC 25], req[xEV BECM DIAG FUNC 26], req[PEV BECM DIAG FUNC 27], or req[xEV BECM DIAG FUNC 86]), and ChrgrInPwMde_D_Actl missing/invalid (req[PEV BECM DIAG FUNC 42] or req[PEV BECM DIAG FUNC 43]), BECM shall used the normal bppc_cell_fail_wload_tmr and bppc_pck_sev_od_v_time_wload, respectively, for instantaneous SOC based cell/pack over discharge protection when contactors are closed
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Warning Light.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Temperature Too Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose battery temperature low per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction Condition: Battery temperature is lower than -40 degree C, calibratable c) Confirmation Time: 100 milliseconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0AFD-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Temperature Too Low b) Set inhibit start for the remaining of the power cycle BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault does not result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Diagnostic monitors exist for corresponding sensor components. b) Failure can be caused by external environment.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack "A" Current Sensor "B" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: at power up, before contactors are closed, and BVREF circuit is not faulted
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose low range pack current sensor offset fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: at power up, before contactors are closed, and BVREF circuit is not faulted b) Malfunction Condition: the reported low range current sensor reading is beyond a calibratable range c) Confirmation Time: 1 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0B0F-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Current Sensor "B" Circuit Range/Performance b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Component is used as part of the diagnostic strategy for other monitored component.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Current Sensor "A"/"B" Correlation

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: 1 second calibratable time after power up, BVREF circuit is not faulted, no offset fault for both high range and low range sensors is present, no out of range fault for high range sensor is present, and the current measurement of both low range and high range current sensor is within the low range current sensor measurement range
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose Current Sensor Implausible per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: 1 second calibratable time after power up, BVREF circuit is not faulted, no offset fault for both high range and low range sensors is present, no out of range fault for high range sensor is present, and the current measurement of both low range and high range current sensor is within the low range current sensor measurement range b) Malfunction Condition: The difference between high range current sensor measurement and low range current sensor measurement is beyond a calibratable threshold c) Confirmation Time: 1 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0B13-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Current Sensor "A"/"B" Correlation b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Component is used as part of the diagnostic strategy for other monitored component.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage Sense "A" Circuit

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose any of the V_CS circuit fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction Condition: HWIO feedback indicates cell voltage sensor Circuit faults include BMIC and OW is confirmed c) Confirmation Time: Event Based Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set corresponding DTC for Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage Sense Circuit b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Perform slow BPO d) Disable cell balancing e) Request the MIL to be illuminated. This is one trip MIL. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Component is used as input to other monitors

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage Sense "W" Circuit

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: BECM shall diagnose DC fast charge contactor voltage sensor fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is measuring port_V per req[BEV BECM HWIO FUNC 08] b) Malfunction Condition: BECM measurement between V_PORT_POS and V_PORT_NEG is out of range high c) Confirmation Time: 1 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0BA9-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage Sense "W" Circuit b) Request the service light to be illuminated The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault does not result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) DC charging is BEV only, no emissions.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage Sense "X" Circuit

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: Sensor measurement circuit is enabled, and the auxiliary contactor is closed
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose V_CONT_AUX circuit fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: Sensor measurement circuit is enabled, and the auxiliary contactor is closed b) Malfunction Condition: the BECM V_CONT_AUX measurement is out of range high or low c) Confirmation Time: 1 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0BAE-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage Sense "X" Circuit b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Component is used as input to other monitors src[CARB 1968.2 (e)(15.1.1)] src[CARB 1968.2 (e)(15.2.1)]
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage Sense "Y" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: Cell voltage and contactor voltage measurements are available, main positive and negative (or auxiliary) contactors are closed, HWIO does not report any unmonitored cell voltages, and V_CONT_POS circuit fault is not present
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose V_CONT_POS measurement rationality fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: Cell voltage and contactor voltage measurements are available, main positive and negative (or auxiliary) contactors are closed, HWIO does not report any unmonitored cell voltages, and V_CONT_POS circuit fault is not present b) Malfunction Condition: the difference between difference between the summation of cell voltages and V_CONT_POS measurement is greater than 50V, calibratable c) Confirmation Time: 5 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0BB4-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage Sense "Y" Circuit Range/Performance b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Component is used as input to other monitors src[CARB 1968.2 (e)(15.1.1)] src[CARB 1968.2 (e)(15.2.1)]
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack State of Charge High

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose battery cell overcharge per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction condition and confirmation time: a. Instantaneous cell maximum cell SOC is greater than 100% for 15 seconds, both calibratable during normal operation (not in charging) b. Maximum cell voltage is greater than 4.45V for 1 second, both calibratable during normal operation (not in charging) c. Maximum cell voltage is greater than 4.4V for 6 seconds, both calibratable during normal operation (not in charging) d. Instantaneous cell maximum cell SOC is greater than 101% for 15 seconds, both calibratable during charging e. Maximum cell voltage is greater than 4.25V for 1 second, both calibratable during charging f. Maximum cell voltage is greater than 4.2V for 2 second, both calibratable during charging g. HWIO feedback indicates cell over charge condition is present, event based Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0C30-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Pack State of Charge High b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Transmit BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = 0x5: Fault if conditions for req[PEV BECM ACCHRG FUNC 05] or req[BEV BECM DCCHRG FUNC 05] is satisfied d) Perform normal BPO e) Request the MIL to be illuminated. This is one trip MIL. BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Failure will result in battery power off, impacts electric motor propulsion. b) Failure disables battery charging
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Warning Light.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Coolant Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: At power up, and the BECM internal key off timer reading is greater than or equal to 21600 seconds (6 hours); and BECM battery pack temperature measurement is valid
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose TBCT sensor rationality fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: At power up, and the BECM internal key off timer reading is greater than or equal to 21600 seconds (6 hours); and BECM battery pack temperature measurement is valid b) Malfunction Condition: The difference between BECM measured battery pack temperature and TBCT measurement is greater than or equal to 28 degree C, calibratable c) Confirmation Time: 1 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0C43-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Coolant Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Range/Performance b) Request service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated. This is a 1-trip MIL. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Failure affects battery thermal management system
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Coolant Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose TBCT sensor circuit faulted low per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction Condition: TBCT measured temperature is lower than -47.27 degree C c) Confirmation Time: 10 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0C44-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Coolant Temperature Sensor "A" Circuit Low b) Request service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Failure affects battery thermal management system
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Coolant Pump "A" Control Circuit Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: CONT_PWR is high
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose TBCP short to ground or open circuit fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: CONT_PWR is high b) Malfunction Condition: the feedback signal BECM receives from TBCP HWIO indicates wire fault short to ground or open circuit c) Confirmation Time: 30 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0C48-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Coolant Pump "A" Control Circuit Low b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. In addition, BECM shall command pump speed to 100% during ODST, and reduce the fault confirmation time to 15 seconds, calibratable. Within the power cycle, if the HWIO indicates the fault is no longer present, BECM shall take 30 seconds maturity time to recover from the fault, stop requesting the service light, and set BattTracClntPump_D_Stat to an appropriate non-fault state. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Component is used as part of the thermal management system b) BECM cannot distinguish the difference between open circuit and short to ground faults
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery System Precharge "A" Time Too Long

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding precharge contactor to be closed, BECM evaluated battery pack voltage is valid, and V_CONT_POS and HtrnAin_UHi_Actl is not faulted
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose precharge contactor stuck open per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding precharge contactor to be closed, BECM evaluated battery pack voltage is valid, and V_CONT_POS and HtrnAin_UHi_Actl is not faulted b) Malfunction Condition: the difference between BECM evaluated battery pack voltage and V_CONT_POS voltage is beyond a calibratable threshold, and the difference between BECM evaluated battery pack voltage and HtrnAin_UHi_Actl is beyond a calibratable threshold c) Confirmation Time: 600 milliseconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0C78-00 Hybrid/EV Battery System Precharge Time Too Long b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Terminate current contactor close sequence d) Inhibit start for the remaining of the power cycle e) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle, if the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Precharge malfunction results in main contactor cannot be closed.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Warning Light.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Temperature Sensor "G" Circuit Range/Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational, and TSL measurement is valid
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose TSL sensor rationality fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational, and TSL measurement is valid b) Malfunction Condition: the difference between the measurement reading of one temperature sensor and the average reading of the remaining temperature sensors is beyond a calibratable range. c) Confirmation Time: 5 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set appropriate DTC BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault does not result in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) No action taken to this fault. b) All Diagnostic Monitors complete normally. c) Charging performance not affected. d) Electric motor propulsion not affected. e) Regen Braking not affected. f) Emissions not affected

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Temperature Sensor "G" Circuit Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose TSL sensor circuit faulted low per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction Condition: the reported temperature sensor reading is lower than a calibratable threshold c) Confirmation Time: 5 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set appropriate DTC BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Charging Current High

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational, and battery pack voltage is valid
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall prevent overcharging battery per the condition defined in def[SRS CHARGE 05]. Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0CA6-00: Hybrid/EV Battery Charging Current High b) Set BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = NotReady The BECM shall clear the fault when ChrgrInPwMde_D_Actl == EvseNotDetected. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Coolant Pump "A" Stuck/Stalled

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: CONT_PWR is high
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose TBCP stall fault per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: CONT_PWR is high b) Malfunction Condition: the feedback signal BECM receives from TBCP HWIO indicates pump stuck/stall fault has occurred c) Confirmation Time: 30 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0CFF-00 Motor Electronics Coolant Pump "A" Stuck/Stalled b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. In addition, BECM shall command pump speed to 100% during ODST, and reduce the fault confirmation time to 15 seconds, calibratable. Within the power cycle, if the HWIO indicates the fault is no longer present, BECM shall take 30 seconds maturity time to recover from the fault, stop requesting the service light, and set BattTracClntPump_D_Stat to an appropriate non-fault state. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Component is used as part of the thermal management system.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Battery Charging System Negative Contactor "A" Stuck Closed

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding auxiliary contactor to be opened, and the conditions listed in req[XEV BECM CONTACT FMEM 09] is not satisfied
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose auxiliary contactor stuck closed per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding auxiliary contactor to be opened, and the conditions listed in req[XEV BECM CONTACT FMEM 09] is not satisfied b) Malfunction Condition: BECM HWIO feedback reports auxiliary contactor is closed c) Confirmation Time: 60 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0D0F-00 Battery Charging System Negative Contactor "A" Stuck Closed b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall clear the fault if the malfunction condition is no longer present. BECM shall carry over the fault between power cycles if the malfunction condition still presents at BECM power off. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Auxiliary contactor fault affects vehicle charging.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Battery Charging System Negative Contactor "A" Stuck Open

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding auxiliary contactor to be closed, battery pack voltage is greater than 75V, calibratable, and AUX_CONT HWIO reports coil current is higher than a calibratable threshold
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose auxiliary contactor stuck open per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding auxiliary contactor to be closed, battery pack voltage is greater than 75V, calibratable, and AUX_CONT HWIO reports coil current is higher than a calibratable threshold b) Malfunction Condition: BECM HWIO feedback reports auxiliary contactor is open, and ChrgrOutHi_U_Actl is outside 10% of battery pack voltage c) Confirmation Time: 2.4 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0D10-00 Battery Charging System Negative Contactor "A" Stuck Open b) Transmit BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = 0x5: Fault if conditions for req[PEV BECM ACCHRG FUNC 05] or req[BEV BECM DCCHRG FUNC 05] is satisfied c) Request the service light to be illuminated d) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle, if the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles; Or BECM shall clear the fault if a quick key cycle has occurred. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Auxiliary contactor malfunction affects vehicle charging.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose AUX_CONT circuit faulted per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational b) Malfunction Condition: AUX_CONT HWIO feedback reports contactor low side driver fault has been detected c) Confirmation Time: 1 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0D11-00 Battery Charging System Negative Contactor "A" Control Circuit/Open b) Request the service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Auxiliary contactor malfunction affects vehicle charging.

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Battery Charging System Negative Contactor "A" Control Circuit Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding Auxiliary contactor to be closed, and BECM evaluated auxiliary contactor status is open
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose AUX_CONT coil current low fault (no power to auxiliary contactor) per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding Auxiliary contactor to be closed, and BECM evaluated auxiliary contactor status is open b) Malfunction Condition: AUX_CONT HWIO reports coil current is lower than a calibratable threshold c) Confirmation Time: 0.4 second (during power up contactor close sequence), or 1.6 seconds (after power up contactor close sequence), both calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0D13-00 Battery Charging System Negative Contactor "A" Control Circuit Low b) Transmit BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = 0x5: Fault if conditions for req[PEV BECM ACCHRG FUNC 05] or req[BEV BECM DCCHRG FUNC 05] is satisfied c) Request the service light to be illuminated d) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Auxiliary contactor malfunction affects vehicle charging.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. DTC.DESCRIPTION: Battery Charger Hybrid/EV Battery Output Power Performance

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: ChrgrRdyStat_D_Actl is not missing and is reporting valid state, BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = Charging and BattChrg_U_Rq > 0 V
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl functional test failed per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: ChrgrRdyStat_D_Actl is not missing and is reporting valid state, BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = Charging and BattChrg_U_Rq > 0 V b) Malfunction Condition: ChrgrRdyStat_D_Actl does not enter Charging state c) Confirmation Time: 5 seconds, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0D5C-00 Battery Charger Hybrid/EV Battery Output Power Performance b) Transmit BattChrgRdyStat_D_Actl = 0x5: Fault if conditions for req[PEV BECM ACCHRG FUNC 05] is satisfied c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met. Specifically for this monitor, the BECM shall latch the pending code over driving cycles, and then set a confirmed code if the monitor condition is satisfied for the second time and the test is failed. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle, if the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles, or BECM shall clear the fault if ChrgrInPwMde_D_Actl transitions to EvseNotDetected for 1 second, calibratable. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Failure terminates vehicle charging. The BECM shall manage each ChrgrSysFaltXX_D_Stat flags as listed in Table 26 independently. The BECM shall treat each ChrgrSysFaltXX_D_Stat = 0x1: Fail flag as a two trip MIL fault counter. BECM shall ignore other ChrgrSysFalXX_D_Stat signals that are not listed in the table.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Cell Balancing Circuit "A" Stuck On

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding cell balancing switch open
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose cell balance circuit stuck closed per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding cell balancing switch open b) Malfunction Condition: HWIO feedback indicates any one cell balance switch within a monitoring IC is stuck closed c) Confirmation Time: 0.1 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set corresponding DTC for the cell balance circuit b) Request service light to be illuminated c) Disable cell balancing d) Reduce both long term and short term charging/discharging power limits to 5kW (FHEV/PHEV) or 5kW (BEV) e) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall clear the fault when HWIO feedback indicates the malfunction is no longer present, without confirmation time. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Cell balancing monitoring is required per CARB regulation
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding cell balancing switch close, and battery pack voltage is higher than a calibratable value

If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Cell balancing monitoring is required per CARB regulation

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Cell Balancing Circuit "H" Stuck On

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding cell balancing switch open
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose cell balance circuit stuck closed per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding cell balancing switch open b) Malfunction Condition: HWIO feedback indicates any one cell balance switch within a monitoring IC is stuck closed c) Confirmation Time: 0.1 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set corresponding DTC for the cell balance circuit b) Request service light to be illuminated c) Disable cell balancing d) Reduce both long term and short term charging/discharging power limits to 5kW (FHEV/PHEV) or 5kW (BEV) e) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall clear the fault when HWIO feedback indicates the malfunction is no longer present, without confirmation time. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Cell balancing monitoring is required per CARB regulation
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Cell Balancing Circuit "H" Stuck Off

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is commanding cell balancing switch close, and battery pack voltage is higher than a calibratable value
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose cell balance circuit stuck open per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is commanding cell balancing switch close, and battery pack voltage is higher than a calibratable value b) Malfunction Condition: HWIO feedback indicates any one cell balance switch within a monitoring IC is stuck open c) Confirmation Time: 0.1 second, calibratable Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set corresponding DTC for the cell balance circuit b) Request service light to be illuminated c) Request the MIL to be illuminated when the MIL setting condition is met The BECM shall clear the fault when HWIO feedback indicates the malfunction is no longer present, without confirmation time. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Cell balancing monitoring is required per CARB regulation
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Service Light(Yellow Wrench).

DTC.DESCRIPTION: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Cell Voltage Low

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Entry Condition: BECM is operational, and battery pack over discharge fault is not present, and cell voltage or soc is valid
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: The BECM shall diagnose battery cell failure per the following strategy: a) Entry Condition: BECM is operational, and battery pack over discharge fault is not present, and cell voltage or soc is valid b) Malfunction Condition: The instantaneous SOC of a battery cell is lower than a calibratable threshold; or battery cell voltage is lower than a calibratable threshold (different thresholds for contactors open and closed scenarios), or HWIO feedback indicates cell over discharge condition is present. c) Confirmation Time: 10 seconds (when contactors are closed), calibratable; or 90 seconds (when contactors are open); or 1 second (regardless of contactor status); or event based (HWIO) Once the fault condition is confirmed, the BECM shall perform the following: a) Set DTC P0DE6-00 Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Cell Voltage Low b) Request the warning light to be illuminated c) Perform normal BPO d) Disable battery cell balancing e) Request the MIL to be illuminated. This is one trip MIL. BECM shall detect this fault during ODST. The BECM shall latch the fault for the current power cycle. If the malfunction condition is present at BECM power off, BECM shall not carry it over power cycles. This fault results in illuminating the MIL per following engineering justifications: a) Failure will results in battery power off, impacts electric motor propulsion. b) Failure disables battery cell balancing system This requirement is a special case for req[XEV BECM DIAG FUNC 115] and req[XEV BECM DIAG FUNC 117]. If BECM detects ChrgrInPwMde_D_Actl = AcBasic || AcDigital || DcCharging, BECM shall use bppc_cell_fail_noload_tmr and bppc_pck_sev_od_v_time_noload, respectively, for instantaneous SOC based cell/pack over discharge protection when contactors are closed. If BECM detects ChrgrInPwMde_D_Actl = AcBasic || AcDigital || DcCharging, and the 10-second average battery current is less than -1A, calibratable, i.e, current is flowing into the battery, BECM shall turn off the instantaneous SOC based cell/pack over discharge protection when contactors are closed. In any other cases, including high range current sensor failure (req[xEV BECM DIAG FUNC 24], req[xEV BECM DIAG FUNC 25], req[xEV BECM DIAG FUNC 26], req[PEV BECM DIAG FUNC 27], or req[xEV BECM DIAG FUNC 86]), and ChrgrInPwMde_D_Actl missing/invalid (req[PEV BECM DIAG FUNC 42] or req[PEV BECM DIAG FUNC 43]), BECM shall used the normal bppc_cell_fail_wload_tmr and bppc_pck_sev_od_v_time_wload, respectively, for instantaneous SOC based cell/pack over discharge protection when contactors are closed
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.ECU_FAULT_ACTION: Battery Power Off/Shut-down. No Charging. No Cell Balancing.
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (Check Engine). Warning Light.
